rebuttals.stab: If it was tourism, the souvenir was a gallows.

rebuttals.theySay: Voter fraud is rampant - millions of illegal votes!
rebuttals.youSay: State audits, DOJ, and AP reviews of the 2020 election found no evidence of fraud at a scale that would change outcomes. Widespread fraud is asserted loudly because it is not proven in court.
rebuttals.stab: If you had millions of illegal votes, you would have millions of convictions. You have vibes.

rebuttals.theySay: The deep state runs everything - elections are fake!
rebuttals.youSay: Career civil servants are constrained by statutes, inspectors general, FOIA, and courts. Conspiracy replaces evidence. If everything is fake, no document can ever convince you - which is the point of the conspiracy.
rebuttals.stab: Deep state is Latin for I lost and need a villain.

rebuttals.theySay: Welfare queens are draining the country dry!
rebuttals.youSay: Census poverty data and GAO program audits show most safety-net spending goes to children, elderly, disabled people, and workers. The welfare queen was a political caricature, not a budget line that explains deficits.
rebuttals.stab: Billionaire tax avoidance dwarfs the grocery budget you are mad about.

rebuttals.theySay: Unions are corrupt and obsolete!
rebuttals.youSay: BLS data show union members earn more and have better benefits on average. Corruption exists in every institution; dissolving worker power is not an anti-corruption plan - it is a management plan.
rebuttals.stab: If unions were powerless, CEOs would not spend so much fighting them.

rebuttals.theySay: Raising the minimum wage kills jobs!
rebuttals.youSay: Empirical reviews find modest minimum-wage increases have small or mixed employment effects while raising earnings for low-wage workers. Absolute claims of mass job death ignore decades of state-level evidence.
rebuttals.stab: Your theory needs a new update; the data already got one.

rebuttals.theySay: Any social program is socialism and look at Venezuela!
rebuttals.youSay: Medicare, Social Security, and public schools are popular American institutions. Venezuela's crisis has specific causes - oil dependence, authoritarianism, sanctions - not the existence of libraries or Medicaid.
rebuttals.stab: Calling fire departments socialism does not make them Venezuelan.

rebuttals.theySay: Democrat cities are crime hellholes!
rebuttals.youSay: FBI and BJS data show crime varies by metro area and has fallen from 1990s peaks in many cities of both parties. Cherry-picking scary clips is not a crime rate. Policy debate needs victimization surveys and UCR trends, not vibes.
rebuttals.stab: If party labels caused crime, rural red counties would be crime-free. They are not.

rebuttals.theySay: Guns don't kill people - people kill people!
rebuttals.youSay: People with easier access to firearms kill more people with firearms. CDC injury data and cross-country comparisons show firearm availability shapes death rates. Tools matter; that is why we regulate cars, planes, and explosives.
rebuttals.stab: People also drive cars. We still require licenses and seatbelts.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: They're mutilating kids with gender ideology!
rebuttals.youSay: Major medical bodies publish careful, evidence-based standards for gender-affirming care; care for minors is limited and regulated. Panic language is designed to ban care, not to cite pediatric endocrinology.
rebuttals.stab: If your argument needs the word mutilation in every sentence, you are doing politics, not medicine.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: They're teaching critical race theory in kindergarten!
rebuttals.youSay: CRT is a graduate-level legal framework. School fights are usually about teaching accurate history of slavery and segregation. Banning books about Rosa Parks is not protecting kids from a law-review seminar.
rebuttals.stab: If a picture book is CRT, then literacy is a conspiracy.

rebuttals.theySay: Parents' rights means schools can't teach anything I dislike!
rebuttals.youSay: Parents have rights; so do other parents and students. Public schools teach a shared curriculum under elected boards and state standards. One parent's veto is not a curriculum.
rebuttals.stab: Your rights end where you demand everyone else's kids erase history.

rebuttals.theySay: The media is all fake news!
rebuttals.youSay: Outlets err and deserve scrutiny. Primary documents - Federal Register, court dockets, FEC filings - still exist. If every reporter is fake, check the PDF yourself. Sunrise cites those PDFs on purpose.
rebuttals.stab: Fake news is easier to yell than FOIA is to file.

rebuttals.theySay: Both parties are the same!
rebuttals.youSay: Parties share donor pressures and failures. Platforms, judicial appointments, voting laws, and climate policy still diverge in measurable ways. Sameness is a refusal to vote or organize, not an analysis.
rebuttals.stab: If they were the same, Project 2025 would not need 900 pages.

rebuttals.theySay: Billionaires earned every penny - leave them alone!
rebuttals.youSay: Wealth at that scale depends on public infrastructure, limited liability, IP law, tax rules, and labor. OpenSecrets and FEC data show political spending protects those rules. Earned is not the same as untaxable.
rebuttals.stab: Nobody builds a billion-dollar fortune on a desert island with no roads or courts.

rebuttals.theySay: Regulation kills freedom!
rebuttals.youSay: Food safety, clean water, banking rules, and workplace standards expand freedom to live without poison and fraud. GAO and EPA document what happens when oversight collapses. Freedom for dumpers is unfreedom for everyone downstream.
rebuttals.stab: Your freedom to dump waste is my kid's asthma attack.

rebuttals.theySay: Democrats wanted to abolish the police!
rebuttals.youSay: Slogan chaos aside, most reform agendas funded mental-health response, accountability, and violence interruption - not literal zero police. Debating budgets requires budget docs, not cable chyron memory.
rebuttals.stab: If abolish was the platform, show me the enacted city ordinance that deleted 911.

rebuttals.theySay: We're sending all our money to Ukraine!
rebuttals.youSay: Aid packages are a small share of the federal budget and often take the form of U.S.-made equipment. Debate the policy - do not invent a fiscal black hole that does not match CBO and CRS scoring.
rebuttals.stab: The deficit is not hiding in Kyiv. Check the tax cuts and interest line items.

rebuttals.theySay: Any criticism of Israeli government policy is antisemitism!
rebuttals.youSay: Antisemitism is real and must be confronted. Criticizing a government's military policy is not hatred of Jews, just as criticizing the U.S. government is not hatred of Americans. Collapse the distinction and you shield policy from scrutiny.
rebuttals.stab: Governments are not religions. Accountability is not bigotry.

rebuttals.theySay: It was just a travel pause, not a Muslim ban!
rebuttals.youSay: Campaign promises targeted Muslim countries; the policy's design and messaging were religiously coded even when lawyers scrubbed the text. Courts and civil-rights groups documented discriminatory intent and impact.
rebuttals.stab: If it walks like a ban and campaigns like a ban, the euphemism is not subtle.

rebuttals.theySay: Asylum seekers are illegal by definition!
rebuttals.youSay: U.S. and international law allow people to request asylum. The claim is processed; it may be granted or denied. Illegal is a political smear for a legal process you dislike.
rebuttals.stab: Calling a court process illegal is how you advertise that you do not like courts.

rebuttals.theySay: Birthright citizenship isn't really in the Constitution!
rebuttals.youSay: The 14th Amendment's text and longstanding interpretation grant citizenship to those born in the U.S. subject to its jurisdiction. Ending it by executive order is a constitutional fight the text does not casually allow.
rebuttals.stab: If you want to amend the 14th, call it an amendment - not a press conference.

rebuttals.theySay: The FBI is irreversibly corrupt!
rebuttals.youSay: The FBI has a documented abusive history - COINTELPRO - and still requires oversight. That is an argument for Church Committee-style accountability, not for declaring every investigation of political violence illegitimate.
rebuttals.stab: Past abuse does not grant you a lifelong FOIA exemption for your coup attempt.

rebuttals.theySay: Trump is the peace president!
rebuttals.youSay: Peace is measured in wars ended, alliances managed, and civilian deaths avoided - not vibes. Score the record with CRS and wire reporting on strikes, withdrawals, and escalations rather than campaign nicknames.
rebuttals.stab: Peace president is a brand. Body counts are a ledger.

rebuttals.theySay: NATO allies are freeloaders!
rebuttals.youSay: Burden-sharing debates are real; alliance free-riding claims often ignore U.S. strategic benefits, basing access, and collective defense value documented in CRS analyses. Insult diplomacy is not a strategy memo.
rebuttals.stab: Calling Canada a freeloader is a heckle, not a national security strategy.

rebuttals.theySay: COVID was a hoax!
rebuttals.youSay: CDC death and hospitalization data, excess mortality studies, and global tallies document a real pandemic. Hoax rhetoric insults grieving families and erases ICU capacity crises hospitals recorded in real time.
rebuttals.stab: A hoax does not fill morgues. A virus does.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: Masks don't work!
rebuttals.youSay: High-quality masks reduce transmission in documented settings; public-health guidance evolved as evidence evolved. Absolute doesn't work erases nuance the same way always wear them outdoors alone did.
rebuttals.stab: Your N95 is not a fashion statement. It is a filter. Filters filter.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: Vaccines have microchips / change your DNA!
rebuttals.youSay: mRNA vaccines do not alter your DNA; CDC and NIH explain the mechanism. Microchip claims are conspiracy content with zero manufacturing evidence. Extraordinary claims need extraordinary receipts - none exist.
rebuttals.stab: If they could microchip you via a shot, they would not need your phone's location permissions.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: School choice is the only education reform that works!
rebuttals.youSay: Families deserve options; draining public schools without accountability can worsen segregation and outcomes. Evidence on vouchers is mixed. Reform that ignores Title I funding and teacher pay is ideology, not a toolkit.
rebuttals.stab: Choice without a funded public baseline is escape hatches for some and leftovers for others.

rebuttals.theySay: College is a scam - just learn a trade!
rebuttals.youSay: Trades are valuable and under-respected. Degree premiums still appear in Census and BLS earnings data on average. The scam is predatory lending and underfunded public colleges - not literacy itself.
rebuttals.stab: You can respect welders without pretending epidemiology is a trade school elective.

rebuttals.theySay: DEI is anti-white racism!
rebuttals.youSay: Diversity programs vary wildly; some are clumsy. Treating any effort to reduce discrimination as racism against the majority flips civil-rights history on its head. Equal opportunity is not reverse oppression.
rebuttals.stab: If inclusion is racism, then the 1964 Civil Rights Act was a hate crime.

rebuttals.theySay: The Great Replacement is real!
rebuttals.youSay: Demographic change is real; a plot to replace white voters via immigration is a white-nationalist conspiracy theory. Fertility, aging, and migration explain population math without a puppet master.
rebuttals.stab: Conspiracy theories turn census tables into enemy battle maps.

rebuttals.theySay: Jan 6 was Antifa false flag!
rebuttals.youSay: Charging documents, geolocation, and guilty pleas identify Trump supporters and militia members. False-flag claims collapse under named defendants and their own livestreams.
rebuttals.stab: Your evidence cannot be a meme while the docket has names.

rebuttals.theySay: Pelosi turned down the National Guard!
rebuttals.youSay: Capitol security failures were real and multi-agency. The claim that one Democratic leader uniquely refused available Guard protection has been repeatedly contradicted by timelines and testimony. Use the January 6 record, not a talking point.
rebuttals.stab: Security failures do not convert a mob into tourists.

rebuttals.theySay: Trump had the best economy ever!
rebuttals.youSay: Pre-pandemic growth continued trends from prior years; unemployment was low, then COVID crashed the economy. Best ever claims ignore who gained (asset owners) and what BLS wage and inequality data show for typical workers.
rebuttals.stab: Dow records are not a wage stub.

rebuttals.theySay: Inflation is entirely Biden's fault!
rebuttals.youSay: Inflation spiked globally after COVID supply shocks and war-related energy prices. U.S. fiscal policy played a role; so did corporate pricing power and global factors. Single-villain economics is cable news, not BLS methodology.
rebuttals.stab: If one president owns global LNG prices, invent a new physics.

rebuttals.theySay: They're banning gas stoves!
rebuttals.youSay: Federal agencies discussed efficiency standards and indoor air research - not a midnight SWAT team for your range. Culture-war ban talk converts technical rulemakings into persecution cosplay.
rebuttals.stab: Reading a proposed rule is harder than posting a frying pan meme.

rebuttals.theySay: ESG is woke capitalism destroying markets!
rebuttals.youSay: Investors price climate and governance risk because disasters and fraud destroy value. Some ESG products are marketing fluff; risk analysis is not a gender studies seminar.
rebuttals.stab: Hurricanes do not check your culture-war scorecard before hitting a port.

rebuttals.theySay: Democrats opened the border on purpose!
rebuttals.youSay: Asylum law, court backlogs, and congressional funding fights shape border management. Open border is a slogan that erases Title 42, expulsions, and detention capacity debates documented in CRS reports.
rebuttals.stab: If the border were open, you would not need a three-hour bridge wait to invent the phrase.

rebuttals.theySay: Migrant caravans are an invasion!
rebuttals.youSay: Invasion is a wartime word. Asylum seekers traveling together for safety are not an army. Military metaphors justify military tactics against civilians.
rebuttals.stab: People walking with kids are not Normandy.

rebuttals.theySay: Christianity is under attack in America!
rebuttals.youSay: Christians remain a large share of the population and hold vast institutional power. Losing monopoly status in public schools or retail greetings is not persecution. Compare to actual religious persecution data worldwide.
rebuttals.stab: Starbucks cups are not the Colosseum.

rebuttals.theySay: There's a war on Christmas!
rebuttals.youSay: Christmas remains a federal holiday saturated in commerce and public celebration. Happy holidays is courtesy, not a tank division. Manufactured victimhood sells outrage ads.
rebuttals.stab: If Christmas is under siege, the mall loudspeakers did not get the memo.

rebuttals.theySay: Only the left cancels people!
rebuttals.youSay: Boycotts and firings happen across the spectrum - book bans, campus speakers, corporate advertisers. The free speech high ground requires defending speech you dislike, not only your team's.
rebuttals.stab: Banning library books is cancel culture with a school-board smile.

rebuttals.theySay: Originalism is just neutral constitutional interpretation!
rebuttals.youSay: Originalism is one contested method. Historians dispute whose original meaning counts, and outcomes often track the justices' politics. Neutral is a brand; methods still require judgment.
rebuttals.stab: If it were a calculator, justices would not split 6-3 so predictably.

rebuttals.theySay: Court expansion is illegitimate court packing!
rebuttals.youSay: Congress has changed the Court's size multiple times. Lifetime seats plus ethics scandals and hardball appointments already politicized the Court. Reform debates are constitutional politics, not sacrilege.
rebuttals.stab: The sacred number nine is tradition, not scripture.

rebuttals.theySay: Impeachments of Trump were illegal coups!
rebuttals.youSay: Impeachment is in the Constitution. The Senate acquitted. A constitutional process you dislike is not a coup; a violent attempt to stop electoral counting is closer to one.
rebuttals.stab: Coups do not come with Senate roll calls and C-SPAN.

rebuttals.theySay: Every investigation is a witch hunt!
rebuttals.youSay: Investigations can be biased and still find real evidence - ask the convictions and plea deals. Witch hunt is a slogan that treats any accountability as persecution.
rebuttals.stab: If every subpoena is a witch hunt, witchcraft is your word for oversight.

rebuttals.theySay: Everyone has classified documents issues!
rebuttals.youSay: Compare cooperation with retrieval, obstruction evidence, and charging decisions. Whataboutism collapses different fact patterns into a shrug. Process and intent matter in Espionage Act cases.
rebuttals.stab: Returning boxes is not the same as hiding boxes.

rebuttals.theySay: Hunter Biden explains every Trump scandal!
rebuttals.youSay: Hunter Biden's legal issues are real and adjudicated separately. They do not erase January 6, classified documents cases, or Project 2025 personnel plans. One family's taxes are not a universal solvent.
rebuttals.stab: Your distraction is not a defense exhibit.

rebuttals.theySay: Project 2025 has nothing to do with Trump!
rebuttals.youSay: Heritage built the playbook with Trump-world authors and alumni; Day One orders tracked its personnel and agency agenda. Distance branding after the election does not erase the resume overlap or the implementation pattern.
rebuttals.stab: If it walks like your transition plan and hires like your transition plan...

rebuttals.theySay: Schedule F just drains the swamp!
rebuttals.youSay: Replacing merit protections with at-will loyalty tests politicizes expertise at EPA, SSA, and nuclear safety shops. GAO and MSPB frameworks exist because patronage produced corruption last time.
rebuttals.stab: Draining the swamp by firing the inspectors is how you get more swamp.

rebuttals.theySay: DOGE is just efficiency - only waste gets cut!
rebuttals.youSay: Efficiency requires metrics, inspectors general, and legal authority. Extra-constitutional access to payment systems and mass RIFs risk cutting capacity citizens need - benefits, food safety, disaster response.
rebuttals.stab: Firing the air-traffic brain trust is not a spreadsheet flex.

rebuttals.theySay: FOIA just slows government down!
rebuttals.youSay: Sunshine is a feature. FOIA.gov documents backlogs that agencies create by underfunding disclosure. If government cannot withstand records requests, it cannot withstand democracy.
rebuttals.stab: Speed without receipts is how scandals stay warm.

rebuttals.theySay: Dark money is free speech!
rebuttals.youSay: Citizens United limited expenditure bans; it did not require secret donors. Voters can evaluate speech better when they know who paid for it. Disclosure is not a gag.
rebuttals.stab: Speech with a bag over its head is lobbying cosplay.

rebuttals.theySay: Lobbyists are just subject-matter experts!
rebuttals.youSay: Expertise is real; paid access is also real. OpenSecrets data show industries buying influence. Public financing and revolving-door bans exist because expertise without sunlight becomes capture.
rebuttals.stab: If it requires a fundraiser to hear your expertise, it is not a seminar.

rebuttals.theySay: States' rights should decide everything!
rebuttals.youSay: States' rights historically defended slavery and Jim Crow. Federal civil rights exist because local majorities abused local minorities. Federalism is a structure, not a moral blank check.
rebuttals.stab: States' rights was the slogan; slavery was the product.

rebuttals.theySay: The Civil War was about states' rights, not slavery!
rebuttals.youSay: Secession documents cite slavery. The Confederacy's own leaders said so. States' rights for slavery is still about slavery.
rebuttals.stab: Read the declarations. They did not secede over tariff poetry.

rebuttals.theySay: Confederate statues are heritage not hate!
rebuttals.youSay: Many monuments were erected during Jim Crow and massive resistance to civil rights - political intimidation in bronze. Heritage that celebrates traitors to the Union in defense of slavery is a choice.
rebuttals.stab: Heritage is a museum. Domination is a pedestal outside a courthouse.

rebuttals.theySay: Every society had slavery - stop picking on America!
rebuttals.youSay: Comparative history is real. America's distinctives include racialized chattel slavery, a Constitution protecting it, a war to end it, and a century of Jim Crow afterward. Everywhere is not an absolution.
rebuttals.stab: Your ancestors' crime does not cancel the ledger; it adds a line.

rebuttals.theySay: Only more police funding stops crime!
rebuttals.youSay: Policing is one tool. Evidence also supports violence interrupters, housing stability, and mental-health crisis teams. Budget maximalism without accountability ignores clearance rates and misconduct costs.
rebuttals.stab: A blank check is not a crime strategy.

rebuttals.theySay: It's a mental health problem, not guns!
rebuttals.youSay: Mental health care needs investment. Most people with mental illness are not violent, and countries with similar mental-health burdens but fewer guns have fewer gun massacres. Both/and beats either/or.
rebuttals.stab: Japan has mental illness. It does not have America's firearm death rate.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: Homeless people choose that lifestyle!
rebuttals.youSay: HUD and Census-linked research tie homelessness to rents, wages, disability, and shelter capacity. Choice narratives erase eviction math.
rebuttals.stab: Nobody chooses frostbite as a lifestyle brand.

sources.title: HUD
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

rebuttals.theySay: Rent control always fails!
rebuttals.youSay: Poorly designed price caps can reduce supply; well-designed stabilization and social housing can protect tenants. Always is ideology. Housing shortages need construction, zoning reform, and tenant protection together.
rebuttals.stab: Your landlord's newsletter is not a peer-reviewed consensus.

rebuttals.theySay: AI means we don't need worker protections anymore!
rebuttals.youSay: Automation shifts bargaining power; it does not erase the need for wages, safety, and benefits. BLS tracks displacement risks. Policy chooses whether gains go to capital only.
rebuttals.stab: Robots do not need weekends. Humans still do.

rebuttals.theySay: TikTok is the only speech platform that matters to ban!
rebuttals.youSay: Foreign data risks deserve scrutiny. Selective panic that ignores other surveillance advertising models is national-security theater. Apply consistent privacy rules, not culture-war bans.
rebuttals.stab: If privacy mattered, half of Silicon Valley would be in the dock too.

rebuttals.theySay: Election deniers are patriots asking questions!
rebuttals.youSay: Questions end when evidence is presented. Endless denial after audits and courts is sabotage of consent of the governed. Patriotism counts votes; it does not invent truckloads of ballots.
rebuttals.stab: Asking questions expired when the answers arrived.

rebuttals.theySay: Both sides gerrymander - so it's fine!
rebuttals.youSay: Both parties have gerrymandered. That argues for independent commissions, not for shrugging while maps dilute voters. Harm is not canceled by reciprocity.
rebuttals.stab: Two getaway cars do not legalize bank robbery.

rebuttals.theySay: The filibuster is sacred!
rebuttals.youSay: The filibuster is a Senate rule, altered many times. It has blocked civil rights and voting rights. Sacred status is nostalgia for gridlock.
rebuttals.stab: A rule that buried anti-lynching bills is not holy water.

rebuttals.theySay: The Electoral College protects small states - popular vote would ruin us!
rebuttals.youSay: The Electoral College can override the national popular vote and overweights some states. National popular vote debates are legitimate democratic design questions, not heresy.
rebuttals.stab: Land does not vote. People do.

rebuttals.theySay: The ACLU only defends the left!
rebuttals.youSay: The ACLU has a long record defending unpopular speech across ideologies, including cases that anger progressives. Disagreement with a case is not proof of cartel capture.
rebuttals.stab: Civil liberties that only cover your team are not civil liberties.

rebuttals.theySay: Fact-checkers are all biased!
rebuttals.youSay: Fact-checkers can err and deserve critique. Primary sources still adjudicate claims. If every referee is biased, publish better evidence - not a boycott of reality.
rebuttals.stab: Refusing measurement does not make your claim taller.

rebuttals.theySay: Teaching history should only make kids proud!
rebuttals.youSay: Honest history includes glory and atrocity. Pride that requires amnesia is propaganda. National Archives documents exist so adults can handle complexity.
rebuttals.stab: If your patriotism needs a redacted textbook, it is fragile.

rebuttals.theySay: Schedule F just fires bad bureaucrats who sabotage the president!
rebuttals.youSay: Schedule Policy/Career and Schedule F convert career experts into at-will staff removable without normal merit protections. GAO and MSPB frameworks exist because politicized firings destroy institutional memory and invite corruption, not because experts are sacred.
rebuttals.stab: If accountability meant competence, you would publish performance metrics, not loyalty oaths.

rebuttals.theySay: DOGE is cutting waste and saving taxpayers billions!
rebuttals.youSay: Federal Register DOGE orders create review powers and hiring freezes; claimed savings require audited baselines. GAO already exists to score waste. Press releases are not audited savings.
rebuttals.stab: If the savings were real, GAO would score them. Screenshots are not audits.

rebuttals.theySay: Tariffs will bring all the factories back overnight!
rebuttals.youSay: Tariffs raise import costs and can reshore some production over years, but BLS and CRS treat them as taxes paid through U.S. prices. Overnight factory magic is campaign poetry, not trade economics.
rebuttals.stab: Factories need workers, power, and capital. A customs invoice is not a groundbreaking ceremony.

rebuttals.theySay: Immigrants are driving a massive crime wave!
rebuttals.youSay: BJS victimization surveys and multiple state analyses do not show immigrants as the engine of a national crime wave. Crime trends move with broader social factors. Anecdotes plus cable chyron is not epidemiology.
rebuttals.stab: If your crime theory needs a foreign accent, it is not a crime theory.

rebuttals.theySay: Asylum is just a loophole for illegals!
rebuttals.youSay: Asylum is a statutory protection rooted in refugee law and international commitments. Backlogs and weak processing capacity are administrative failures, not proof that protection is fake.
rebuttals.stab: Calling a right a loophole is how you erase the statute without amending it.

rebuttals.theySay: Birthright citizenship was a mistake we can erase by EO!
rebuttals.youSay: The 14th Amendment's citizenship clause and long Supreme Court interpretation do not vanish because an executive order wants a different politics. Constitutional text is not a press secretary memo.
rebuttals.stab: If an EO can rewrite the 14th Amendment, we do not have a Constitution. We have a mood ring.

rebuttals.theySay: Climate scientists are in it for the grant money!
rebuttals.youSay: NOAA and IPCC assessments rest on multiple independent observational records, not one lab's paycheck. Conspiracy requires explaining why oil majors' own scientists also documented warming for decades.
rebuttals.stab: Your uncle's Facebook post is not a peer-reviewed temperature record.

rebuttals.theySay: EV mandates are government tyranny against cars!
rebuttals.youSay: Emissions standards and fleet rules are ordinary Clean Air Act tools. Markets already shift under fuel costs and tech change. Calling every pollution rule tyranny empties the word.
rebuttals.stab: Seatbelts were tyranny too, until the funeral rate dropped.

rebuttals.theySay: The Paris Agreement does nothing - leaving was smart!
rebuttals.youSay: Paris is a framework for nationally determined contributions and transparency. Leaving reduces U.S. leverage and signals free-rider preference. Doing nothing is not a climate strategy.
rebuttals.stab: Walking out of the fire drill does not put out the fire.

rebuttals.theySay: COVID was overblown - just a flu!
rebuttals.youSay: CDC excess-death and hospitalization data showed a multi-year mortality shock far beyond seasonal flu baselines. Minimizing mass death is politics, not epidemiology.
rebuttals.stab: If it was just a flu, the cemeteries would not have needed overflow plans.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: Vaccines do more harm than good!
rebuttals.youSay: CDC and NIH track adverse events and still find benefits dwarfing risks for routine immunizations. Rare harms are monitored precisely because public health systems measure them.
rebuttals.stab: Anecdotes are not denominators. Public health runs on rates.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: Medicaid work requirements just build character!
rebuttals.youSay: Many Medicaid enrollees already work, caretake, or have disabilities. Work paperwork requirements mainly generate coverage loss through red tape, not character transformation.
rebuttals.stab: Character is not measured by how many forms you lose before chemo.

rebuttals.theySay: Abortion should be left entirely to the states!
rebuttals.youSay: Dobbs returned regulation to states, producing bans that override majorities in some places and force interstate travel for care. A right that ends at a state line is conditional liberty.
rebuttals.stab: Your ZIP code is not a moral philosophy.

rebuttals.theySay: All gender-affirming care is mutilation!
rebuttals.youSay: Major medical bodies distinguish adult care, adolescent protocols, and banned conversion practices. Blanket mutilation rhetoric erases clinical guidelines and parental/patient consent frameworks.
rebuttals.stab: If every surgery you dislike is mutilation, cancel sports medicine next.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: They are teaching CRT in kindergarten!
rebuttals.youSay: Critical race theory is a law-school framework. K-12 fights are usually about whether classrooms may discuss slavery, segregation, and racism using primary sources. Archives exist for a reason.
rebuttals.stab: If reading the Emancipation Proclamation is CRT, your complaint is with 1863.

rebuttals.theySay: Parents rights means banning books and curricula!
rebuttals.youSay: Parents already have huge sway via boards and opt-outs. Blanket bans that purge library shelves for everyone are majoritarian censorship, not parental choice.
rebuttals.stab: Your parental right ends where you start parenting the whole district's bookshelf.

rebuttals.theySay: Teachers unions destroyed public schools!
rebuttals.youSay: School outcomes track segregation, poverty, and funding gaps documented by Census and education research. Blaming only unions ignores decades of unequal investment.
rebuttals.stab: If unions alone caused failure, wealthy union districts would look like ruins.

rebuttals.theySay: College is a scam - nobody should go!
rebuttals.youSay: BLS earnings premiums for degree holders remain large on average, while debt and underfunding are real policy failures. The scam frame collapses unequal public investment into anti-education nihilism.
rebuttals.stab: Fix the price of college. Do not celebrate ignorance as a vibe.

rebuttals.theySay: DEI is illegal anti-white discrimination!
rebuttals.youSay: Equal employment law already bans racial discrimination. Many DEI programs are outreach, bias training, or compliance. Treating every equity office as a racial conspiracy is culture-war expansion of SFFA beyond campuses.
rebuttals.stab: If fairness offends you only when the beneficiaries are not you, it was never about fairness.

rebuttals.theySay: A woke military cannot win wars!
rebuttals.youSay: Readiness is about training, maintenance, recruiting, and strategy. Culture-war branding is not a force assessment. Pentagon metrics live in budgets and GAO readiness work, not memes.
rebuttals.stab: Empires fall from logistics and politics, not rainbow stickers on a briefing slide.

rebuttals.theySay: NATO allies are freeloaders - ditch them!
rebuttals.youSay: Alliance burden-sharing is contested, but collective defense multiplies U.S. power projection. Walking away hands adversaries a fractured Europe. Accounting debates are not abandonment arguments.
rebuttals.stab: You do not cancel the fire department because a neighbor owns a nicer hose.

rebuttals.theySay: Foreign aid is a huge waste of our budget!
rebuttals.youSay: Foreign assistance is a small slice of federal spending. Cutting it can raise refugee, conflict, and pandemic spillover costs. GAO can score waste without treating diplomacy as theft.
rebuttals.stab: Pennies on the federal dollar are not why your town lacks a clinic.

rebuttals.theySay: Any criticism of Israel is antisemitism!
rebuttals.youSay: Antisemitism is real and must be fought. Criticism of a government's military or settlement policy is not inherently hatred of Jews, just as criticizing Egypt is not anti-Arab racism.
rebuttals.stab: Governments are not religions. Conflating them protects neither Jews nor Palestinians.

rebuttals.theySay: January 6 was an FBI fedsurrection!
rebuttals.youSay: Thousands of hours of video, charging docs, and convictions show a real mob attacking police and Congress. Informants exist in many cases; they do not erase the breach you can watch.
rebuttals.stab: If it was mostly feds, your friends in the zip-tie photos need better agents.

rebuttals.theySay: Trump actually won 2020 - it was stolen!
rebuttals.youSay: Sixty-plus failed lawsuits, state audits, and AP reviews found no fraud at outcome-changing scale. Losing under rules you liked until you lost is not theft.
rebuttals.stab: If you cannot prove it in court, it is a podcast, not a presidency.

rebuttals.theySay: Voter ID is just common sense!
rebuttals.youSay: ID rules can be crafted fairly, but many bills add narrow IDs, purge rules, and polling cuts that Brennan Center and election administrators flag as turnout barriers. Common sense is access plus security, not a obstacle course.
rebuttals.stab: If the goal is security, fund free IDs and more poll sites. If the goal is fewer voters, you already showed your hand.

rebuttals.theySay: Mail ballots are fraud machines!
rebuttals.youSay: States have used absentee voting for decades with bipartisan acceptance for military voters. Fraud rates documented in audits remain tiny. Convenience is not conspiracy.
rebuttals.stab: Soldiers voting from abroad somehow became fraud only when your candidate lost.

rebuttals.theySay: The Electoral College protects small states!
rebuttals.youSay: It weights voters unequally by design. Small-state protection can be debated; calling unequal vote power sacred democracy is branding, not civics.
rebuttals.stab: One person, one vote is not radical. It is the slogan you use until the math fails you.

rebuttals.theySay: Both sides gerrymander - stop complaining!
rebuttals.youSay: Both parties have gerrymandered; that does not make extreme maps democratic. Independent commissions and court limits exist because voters should choose politicians, not the reverse.
rebuttals.stab: Both sides do it is a confession, not a defense.

rebuttals.theySay: The filibuster is what saves the republic!
rebuttals.youSay: The modern talking-point filibuster is a Senate rule, not a constitutional commandment. It blocks voting rights and climate bills while exceptions appear when majorities want tax cuts.
rebuttals.stab: Sacred until the tax bill, disposable after: that is not principle.

rebuttals.theySay: The Supreme Court is just calling balls and strikes!
rebuttals.youSay: Courts interpret open-textured clauses with methods that change outcomes on abortion, money in politics, and agency power. Neutrality claims do not erase the political stakes of appointments.
rebuttals.stab: If it were only balls and strikes, confirmation hearings would be five minutes long.

rebuttals.theySay: Originalism is the only objective method!
rebuttals.youSay: Historians disagree about founding-era meanings, and selective originalism often ignores founding practices that are politically inconvenient. Method branding is not a time machine.
rebuttals.stab: If originalism were a science, originalists would not split into factions every term.

rebuttals.theySay: Killing Chevron deference frees America from bureaucrats!
rebuttals.youSay: Chevron let expert agencies fill statutory gaps Congress left. Ending it shifts power to generalist judges. That is a governance choice, not automatic liberty.
rebuttals.stab: You did not drain the swamp. You moved it into chambers with life tenure.

rebuttals.theySay: Corporations are people - money is speech!
rebuttals.youSay: Citizens United treated independent expenditures as protected speech. Corporate legal personhood for contracts is not the same as equal political citizenship. Voters without megaphones lose.
rebuttals.stab: My corporation cannot be drafted, married, or jailed. Funny kind of person.

rebuttals.theySay: Minimum wage hikes always kill jobs!
rebuttals.youSay: BLS and a large empirical literature find mixed, often modest employment effects depending on level and phase-in. Always kills jobs is ideology with a spreadsheet costume.
rebuttals.stab: If wages must stay 1990 forever, call it a museum, not a labor market.

rebuttals.theySay: Unions are all corrupt bosses!
rebuttals.youSay: Unions are democratic organizations with legal duties and public LM filings. Corruption exists in every sector; BLS still shows a union wage premium. Anecdote is not the median member.
rebuttals.stab: You hate dues more than you hate the CEO's jet. Be honest.

rebuttals.theySay: Right-to-work laws protect worker freedom!
rebuttals.youSay: Right-to-work bans fair-share fees, weakening unions that still must represent free riders. Freedom for free riders is often employer strategy dressed as liberty.
rebuttals.stab: If it is about freedom, why do the same bills never boost organizing rights?

rebuttals.theySay: Tax cuts always pay for themselves!
rebuttals.youSay: CBO and CRS score dynamic effects; they do not show large tax cuts routinely self-financing. Deficits after major cuts are a pattern, not a coincidence.
rebuttals.stab: If tax cuts paid for themselves, we would be drowning in surplus. We are not.

rebuttals.theySay: The IRS is a weapon against patriots!
rebuttals.youSay: Tax administration audits high-income complexity and refundable credits. Politicized targeting is illegal and investigated when alleged. Defunding enforcement mainly helps sophisticated evasion.
rebuttals.stab: If your patriotism depends on nobody checking the return, it is a business model.

rebuttals.theySay: Homelessness is just drugs and personal failure!
rebuttals.youSay: HUD and Census data show housing cost burdens and shortages. Addiction and mental illness interact with rents. Personal failure does not build apartments.
rebuttals.stab: You cannot AA your way out of a 2-bedroom that costs half your paycheck.

sources.title: HUD
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

rebuttals.theySay: Democrats are soft on crime and cities burned!
rebuttals.youSay: BJS and FBI trend data show complex rises and falls that do not map cleanly onto one party's slogan. Soft on crime is a campaign chant that skips victimization survey nuance.
rebuttals.stab: If cable news is your crime dashboard, you are the product, not the analyst.

rebuttals.theySay: Support the badge - police are never the problem!
rebuttals.youSay: Most officers are not murderers; accountability for the ones who are is how you protect the profession. DOJ pattern-or-practice cases exist because some departments rot.
rebuttals.stab: A badge is not a halo. Treat it like power that needs oversight.

rebuttals.theySay: More guns always mean less crime!
rebuttals.youSay: CDC and research literature show firearms raise lethality of arguments, suicides, and accidents even where defensive uses occur. Always is not a dataset.
rebuttals.stab: If more guns automatically meant safety, the ER would be a spa.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: The media is the enemy of the people!
rebuttals.youSay: Watchdog reporting exposes corruption; authoritarian movements always brand scrutiny as treason. Read primary documents. Enemy of the people is a purge phrase.
rebuttals.stab: If every critical headline is treason, you are not seeking truth. You are seeking a throne.

rebuttals.theySay: Big Tech only censors conservatives!
rebuttals.youSay: Platforms moderate across ideologies under opaque rules; conservatives also dominate parts of social media. The antidote is transparency and competition policy, not a state speech police for your side.
rebuttals.stab: Wanting the algorithm to favor you is not a free speech principle.

rebuttals.theySay: But her emails! Why the double standard?
rebuttals.youSay: Email server practices were investigated. They do not cancel later classified mishandling cases, Jan 6, or current executive actions. Past scandals are not lifetime immunity cards.
rebuttals.stab: You cannot run a country on unfinished 2016 Facebook comments.

rebuttals.theySay: Both parties are the same - nothing matters!
rebuttals.youSay: Parties share donor capture problems and differ sharply on voting rights, climate, labor, and abortion. Cynicism that erases those differences is how minority agendas win without persuading you.
rebuttals.stab: If nothing matters, only the people who still show up matter. Guess who that helps.

rebuttals.theySay: America was never a racist country!
rebuttals.youSay: National Archives documents slavery, Jim Crow, redlining, and exclusion acts. A country can contain democracy and white supremacy at once. Denial is not patriotism.
rebuttals.stab: If it was never racist, explain the separate water fountain without using the word.

rebuttals.theySay: The Civil War was not about slavery!
rebuttals.youSay: Secession declarations and Confederate leaders named slavery as central. States rights was the right to own people. Library of Congress primary texts settle this.
rebuttals.stab: When the documents say slavery, believe the documents.

rebuttals.theySay: Reparations are impossible and unfair!
rebuttals.youSay: Feasibility is a design question: tax credits, baby bonds, targeted investment. Unfair ignores unpaid labor, redlining, and wealth gaps Census still measures. Hard is not the same as unjustified.
rebuttals.stab: We built the interstate system. We can build a spreadsheet.

rebuttals.theySay: Freedom to Fix just lets you repair your own car - why do greens hate that?
rebuttals.youSay: The June 2026 memorandum directs EPA to expand aftermarket emissions pathways and deprioritize certain tampering enforcement. DIY repairs are not the same as weakening Clean Air Act practical controls. Read the memo, not the slogan.
rebuttals.stab: If it were only about wrenches, it would not need an EPA enforcement deprioritization clause.

rebuttals.theySay: Presidents use emergency powers all the time - stop freaking out!
rebuttals.youSay: Frequency is not the same as health. CRS and congressional war-powers literature treat emergency authorities as exceptions that expand when Congress fails to constrain them. Normalizing forever emergencies is how exceptions eat the rulebook.
rebuttals.stab: If everything is an emergency, nothing is oversight.

rebuttals.theySay: NSPMs are just internal memos - they are not laws!
rebuttals.youSay: National Security Presidential Memoranda can bind agencies without the transparency of statutes or ordinary rulemaking. Internal is not the same as unimportant when the subject is security state priorities.
rebuttals.stab: Secret priority lists shape budgets and targeting. Call them memos if it helps you sleep.

rebuttals.theySay: The pardon power is absolute so any pardon is fine!
rebuttals.youSay: The Constitution grants broad clemency, not a morality shield. Patterns of self-dealing or allied impunity are still fair game for voters, Congress, and historians even when courts cannot unwind a pardon.
rebuttals.stab: Absolute power is exactly when you judge the character of the person using it.

rebuttals.theySay: Adjusting aircraft imports protects American aerospace jobs!
rebuttals.youSay: Trade proclamations can shift costs across airlines, manufacturers, and passengers. CRS and BLS treat tariff and import adjustments as distributional choices, not automatic job miracles.
rebuttals.stab: A proclamation is not a hiring fair. Follow the price of tickets and parts.

rebuttals.theySay: A fertilizer emergency proves the president is fighting for farmers!
rebuttals.youSay: Emergency trade relief can help farm inputs and also expands the habit of governing by proclamation. Ask for duration, alternatives, and whether Congress was cut out - not just the ribbon-cutting photo.
rebuttals.stab: If every commodity spike is an emergency, Congress is a museum exhibit.

rebuttals.theySay: Restoring Pacific fishing is common-sense American jobs!
rebuttals.youSay: Ocean access proclamations trade off conservation science, treaty obligations, and commercial harvest. Common sense without stock assessments is a slogan wearing waders.
rebuttals.stab: Fish stocks do not care about your press release.

rebuttals.theySay: Schedule Policy/Career is just efficiency!
rebuttals.youSay: Converting policy-influencing roles to at-will status trades expertise for loyalty. GAO and MSPB frameworks exist because politicized removals destroy continuity and invite corruption.
rebuttals.stab: Efficiency that needs a loyalty filter is a purge with a stopwatch.

rebuttals.theySay: Career civil servants are the deep state!
rebuttals.youSay: Career staff implement statutes Congress passed. Calling expertise a conspiracy is how you justify firing the people who know where the bodies are buried in the budget.
rebuttals.stab: If competence is a coup, you are not fighting tyranny. You are fighting homework.

rebuttals.theySay: FOIA is weaponized against conservatives!
rebuttals.youSay: FOIA is a statute that applies across administrations. Backlogs and denials are documented government-wide. Transparency that only feels fair when your side wins is not transparency.
rebuttals.stab: If sunlight burns only your opponents, you were not standing in principle. You were standing in shade.

rebuttals.theySay: Dark money is free speech!
rebuttals.youSay: Speech is not the same as secret bankrolling. FEC disclosures and OpenSecrets tracking exist because voters deserve to know who is buying the megaphone.
rebuttals.stab: If your ideas need a mask and a shell LLC, they are not winning on merit.

rebuttals.theySay: Voter ID is the only election reform we need!
rebuttals.youSay: ID rules can be designed fairly or as obstacle courses. Brennan Center research after Shelby County shows how restrictive packages stack with purged rolls and closed polling places.
rebuttals.stab: If fraud were the epidemic, courts would be drowning in proven cases. They are not.

rebuttals.theySay: CISA is a censorship agency!
rebuttals.youSay: CISA's election-security role is infrastructure resilience and threat information. Conflating cybersecurity coordination with content policing is a category error that weakens election admin.
rebuttals.stab: Patching a county server is not editing your Facebook meme.

rebuttals.theySay: Climate change is real but not urgent!
rebuttals.youSay: NOAA observations and IPCC assessments show rising damages, extremes, and locked-in risk with delay. Not urgent usually means not urgent for people who can buy higher ground.
rebuttals.stab: The atmosphere does not wait for your quarterly earnings call.

rebuttals.theySay: EPA overreach always kills jobs!
rebuttals.youSay: Clean air and water rules have costs and benefits; EPA and academic literature document health savings that do not appear in a plant's quarterly memo. Always is not an impact analysis.
rebuttals.stab: Asthma ER visits are an economic cost whether your lobbyist counts them or not.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: Renewables are too unreliable to matter!
rebuttals.youSay: Grid planning already mixes firm, flexible, and storage resources. Unreliable is a slogan that ignores interconnection queues, transmission, and falling costs documented across energy agencies.
rebuttals.stab: The sun is intermittent. So is a coal plant on outage. Plan the system.

rebuttals.theySay: Oil dominance is energy freedom!
rebuttals.youSay: Fossil dominance exports price shocks and climate risk. Freedom that depends on volatile global commodities is a leash with a patriotic paint job.
rebuttals.stab: If a foreign cartel can spike your gas price, you are not free. You are exposed.

rebuttals.theySay: Medicaid work requirements just promote personal responsibility!
rebuttals.youSay: Paperwork barriers kick eligible people off coverage even when they work irregular hours. CBO and health-services research treat churn as a feature of red tape, not a virtue.
rebuttals.stab: Responsibility that starts with a lost pharmacy claim is cruelty with a worksheet.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: Obamacare failed and premiums prove it!
rebuttals.youSay: The ACA expanded coverage and banned preexisting-condition exclusions; premium fights are often about subsidy design and insurer markets. Failed usually means unfinished, not nonexistent.
rebuttals.stab: If it failed so hard, why is repealing preexisting-condition protections still a third rail?

rebuttals.theySay: Medicare Advantage is better than traditional Medicare!
rebuttals.youSay: Private plans can offer extras while billing the government more. GAO and MedPAC work repeatedly flag overpayments and prior-auth friction. Better for whom is the whole question.
rebuttals.stab: If it needs heavy marketing and network traps, ask who the product is.

rebuttals.theySay: Realigning the childhood vaccine schedule is just following other countries!
rebuttals.youSay: Peer-country cherry-picking is not the same as transparent evidence review. CDC processes exist so recommendations track data, not executive branding.
rebuttals.stab: Science by press office is how confidence dies.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: The CDC is deep-state health tyranny!
rebuttals.youSay: Public-health agencies publish methods and surveillance data you can audit. Tyranny is a word that loses meaning when applied to outbreak tables.
rebuttals.stab: If a case-count dashboard is tyranny, your dictionary needs a vaccine.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: Dobbs returned abortion to the people - debate over!
rebuttals.youSay: Dobbs returned abortion to state legislatures, not to pregnant people as moral agents. Ballot fights, travel bans, and ER refusals show the debate intensified.
rebuttals.stab: The people include the patient in the waiting room, not only the lobbyist in the gallery.

rebuttals.theySay: Crime dropped because Trump is tough!
rebuttals.youSay: BJS and FBI trend data move with many factors across jurisdictions and years. Single-cause victory laps ignore baseline trends and local policy mix.
rebuttals.stab: Correlation with a rally chant is not a causal model.

rebuttals.theySay: Immigrants drain welfare and bankrupt states!
rebuttals.youSay: Eligibility rules already bar many noncitizens from major federal benefits. Census and budget analyses do not support a simple bankrupt-the-treasury cartoon.
rebuttals.stab: If the math needed a scapegoat, it was never math.

rebuttals.theySay: The border is the only issue that matters!
rebuttals.youSay: Border policy matters and so do wages, healthcare, climate, and voting rights. Single-issue tunnel vision is how you get stampeded past everything else on the agenda.
rebuttals.stab: A country is bigger than a fence line.

rebuttals.theySay: Mass deportation is logistically easy and popular!
rebuttals.youSay: Logistics include courts, detention capacity, employer dependency, and family separation. Easy in a speech is not easy in a democracy with due process.
rebuttals.stab: If it were easy, prior administrations would have finished the spreadsheet.

rebuttals.theySay: Sanctuary cities are lawless!
rebuttals.youSay: Local priorities about civil detainers are not nullification of criminal law. Cities still prosecute crimes; they limit becoming a federal immigration dragnet.
rebuttals.stab: Lawless is a word campaigns use when local democracy disagrees.

rebuttals.theySay: Teachers are indoctrinating kids with ideology!
rebuttals.youSay: State standards, elected boards, and parental rights already structure curricula. Indoctrination is often the label for teaching histories and identities someone wants erased.
rebuttals.stab: If a worksheet about Reconstruction is grooming, your fear needs a hobby.

rebuttals.theySay: DEI is illegal discrimination!
rebuttals.youSay: Equal opportunity programs vary widely; some are lawful inclusion efforts and some overreach. Blanket bans treat diversity paperwork as the same as Jim Crow.
rebuttals.stab: If your civil-rights theory starts from resentment of pronouns, you are not reading Title VII.

rebuttals.theySay: College is a scam - skip it!
rebuttals.youSay: Returns vary by major and institution; Census and BLS still show average wage premia. Scam is a blunt word for a financing and sorting problem that needs reform, not nihilism.
rebuttals.stab: Burning the ladder is not the same as fixing the toll.

rebuttals.theySay: Student debt is personal failure!
rebuttals.youSay: Tuition growth, state disinvestment, and wage polarization shaped the debt load. Personal budgeting did not invent the price of a public university.
rebuttals.stab: You cannot side-hustle your way out of a policy choice.

rebuttals.theySay: Parents rights mean parents control everything taught!
rebuttals.youSay: Parents have rights; so do other parents, students, and public institutions bound by civil-rights law. Absolute control by the loudest faction is not pluralism.
rebuttals.stab: Public school is not your group chat admin panel.

rebuttals.theySay: Jan 6 was a fed-surrection!
rebuttals.youSay: Court records, video, and guilty pleas document thousands of participants. A few informants do not erase a crowd that built a gallows and stormed the Capitol.
rebuttals.stab: If every crime you dislike is a false flag, you have left evidence for cosplay.

rebuttals.theySay: Trump always brings peace!
rebuttals.youSay: Peace claims require outcomes: wars ended, alliances stabilized, civilian harm reduced. Slogans about peace while expanding force authorities are marketing.
rebuttals.stab: A peace brand is not a ceasefire.

rebuttals.theySay: NATO is obsolete!
rebuttals.youSay: Alliances are cost-sharing machines against coercion. Obsolete usually means inconvenient for a bilateral deal-making style, not a serious reading of European security.
rebuttals.stab: If Putin cheers your take, check your homework.

rebuttals.theySay: Any criticism of Israel is antisemitism!
rebuttals.youSay: Antisemitism is real and rising; criticizing a government's military policy is not automatically hatred of Jews. Collapsing the two shields both bigots and bad policy from scrutiny.
rebuttals.stab: If a state cannot be criticized, it is not an ally in a democracy. It is a golden calf.

rebuttals.theySay: China is the only threat that matters!
rebuttals.youSay: Strategic competition with China is real; so are climate, pandemics, and domestic democratic decay. Monomania is how you miss the other fires.
rebuttals.stab: A serious strategy can walk and chew gum. A slogan cannot.

rebuttals.theySay: Unions are obsolete in the modern economy!
rebuttals.youSay: BLS still finds a union wage premium. Modern work did not abolish bargaining power problems; it relocated them to apps and contractors.
rebuttals.stab: Obsolete to whom - the worker or the shareholder presentation?

rebuttals.theySay: Gig workers only want flexibility, not employment rights!
rebuttals.youSay: Surveys show many want both flexibility and benefits. Misclassification fights are about who pays for risk when the app sets the price.
rebuttals.stab: Flexibility without a sick day is a brand name for precarity.

rebuttals.theySay: Corporate tax cuts unlock investment and wages!
rebuttals.youSay: CBO and empirical reviews show buybacks and shareholder returns often dominate. Investment and wages need demand, competition, and bargaining power - not only a lower rate.
rebuttals.stab: If the cut always becomes a yacht, it was not a jobs program.

rebuttals.theySay: Deficits are only a Democratic problem!
rebuttals.youSay: CBO baselines show deficits under both parties depending on tax cuts, wars, downturns, and pandemics. Selective deficit hawkishness is a tell.
rebuttals.stab: Math does not care about your yard sign.

rebuttals.theySay: Inflation is only caused by government spending!
rebuttals.youSay: BLS CPI tracks multiple drivers: supply shocks, housing, energy, corporate pricing power. Only spending is a monocausal story for a multicausal index.
rebuttals.stab: If your inflation theory has one villain, it is a campaign ad.

rebuttals.theySay: People choose the streets!
rebuttals.youSay: HUD point-in-time counts and housing-cost data show scarcity and rents. Addiction and illness interact with housing; choice rhetoric erases the price of a key.
rebuttals.stab: Nobody chooses frostbite for the aesthetic.

sources.title: HUD
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

rebuttals.theySay: Only more police funding solves crime!
rebuttals.youSay: Policing is one tool beside lighting, housing, jobs, and prosecutors. BJS victimization trends do not show a single-lever utopia.
rebuttals.stab: A badge is not a housing voucher with a siren.

sources.title: HUD
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

rebuttals.theySay: It is mental health, not guns!
rebuttals.youSay: Mental health care access matters and still does not explain U.S. firearm death rates versus peer countries. CDC data keep pointing at lethality of means.
rebuttals.stab: Other countries have depression. They do not have our funeral schedule.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: Supreme Court ethics rules are already enough!
rebuttals.youSay: A code without strong investigation and enforcement is etiquette. Public confidence surveys and gift scandals show the gap between parchment and practice.
rebuttals.stab: If the honor system worked, we would not be reading about the yacht.

rebuttals.theySay: Originalism solves every hard case objectively!
rebuttals.youSay: Historians disagree, and selective history is still a choice. Originalism is a method with factions, not a vending machine that dispenses one answer.
rebuttals.stab: If the Founders had one mind, they would not have needed amendments.

rebuttals.theySay: Killing Chevron restored liberty from bureaucrats!
rebuttals.youSay: Chevron deference let expert agencies fill statutory gaps Congress left. Courts still need to interpret law; the shift reallocates power toward judges and litigants with resources.
rebuttals.stab: Liberty for the best-lawyered party is not the same as liberty for the public.

rebuttals.theySay: The press failed so I only trust influencers!
rebuttals.youSay: Newsrooms fail and still beat anonymous telegram channels on corrections culture. Influencers rarely publish methods or FOIA logs.
rebuttals.stab: If your news diet has no editors, you are the product and the mark.

rebuttals.theySay: Both sides are equally violent!
rebuttals.youSay: Political violence is wrong from anyone; datasets and court records do not show perfect symmetry in every period. Equal blame is often a way to avoid naming the bigger threat.
rebuttals.stab: If your scale always reads 50-50, it is broken.

rebuttals.theySay: America is the greatest - stop criticizing it!
rebuttals.youSay: Loving a country includes reading its archives. National Parks and National Archives publish hard histories because denial is not strength.
rebuttals.stab: A team that bans film study is not building a dynasty.

rebuttals.theySay: We already learned all the history that matters!
rebuttals.youSay: New documents, archaeology, and scholarship keep revising understanding. Curriculum fights prove history is alive - and politically useful.
rebuttals.stab: If history were finished, lobbyists would not be rewriting the standards.

rebuttals.theySay: Proof of citizenship for voting only stops illegal votes!
rebuttals.youSay: Documentary proof requirements block eligible citizens who lack ready paperwork, especially naturalized, elderly, and low-income voters. Noncitizen voting remains vanishingly rare in audited studies. Election security is real; disenfranchising paperwork traps are a different project.
rebuttals.stab: If the problem is rare, the solution should not be a mass paperwork tax on citizens.

sources.title: Ensuring Citizenship Verification and Integrity in Federal Elections

rebuttals.theySay: DEI is illegal discrimination against white people!
rebuttals.youSay: Equity programs that expand opportunity are not the same as racial quotas banned by courts. Contractor DEI bans use discrimination language to chill voluntary inclusion work that never guaranteed anyone a job over a more qualified applicant.
rebuttals.stab: Calling inclusion discrimination does not make exclusion colorblind.

sources.title: Addressing DEI Discrimination by Federal Contractors
Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
Students for Fair Admissions v. Harvard

rebuttals.theySay: TrumpIRA.gov proves this administration helps workers save!
rebuttals.youSay: Branding a federal retirement portal after a president is marketing. Worker retirement security depends on wages, fees, Social Security, and employer plans, not a vanity domain. Check the contribution rules and consumer protections, not the logo.
rebuttals.stab: A .gov with a campaign name is not a raise.

sources.title: Promoting Retirement-Savings Access for American Workers by Establishing TrumpIRA.gov

rebuttals.theySay: A mental-health executive order proves guns are not the issue!
rebuttals.youSay: Serious mental illness treatment access matters. It does not erase firearm mortality data, background-check gaps, or the fact that most gun deaths are not mass shootings by people with diagnosed serious mental illness. Both public health tools can be true.
rebuttals.stab: Treatment is good. Using it as a shield against gun policy is a dodge.

sources.title: Accelerating Medical Treatments for Serious Mental Illness

rebuttals.theySay: The president must save college sports!
rebuttals.youSay: Campus athletics finance and Title IX compliance are real policy fights. Declaring urgent national action by executive order is culture-war federalization of NCAA politics, not a substitute for legislation or conference governance.
rebuttals.stab: If football needs a national emergency, so does student debt.

sources.title: Urgent National Action To Save College Sports

rebuttals.theySay: Ending de minimis duty-free imports protects American workers!
rebuttals.youSay: Closing the de minimis loophole can address underpriced imports and fentanyl packaging concerns. It also raises consumer prices and customs friction. Trade-offs exist; pretending it is costless patriotism is marketing.
rebuttals.stab: Tariffs are taxes. Someone pays them at checkout.

sources.title: Continuing the Suspension of Duty-Free De Minimis Treatment for All Countries

rebuttals.theySay: Ending some tariffs shows flexible genius dealmaking!
rebuttals.youSay: Selective termination of IEEPA duties while keeping other surcharges shows trade policy as an executive toggle, not a stable rules-based system businesses can plan around. Volatility is a cost.
rebuttals.stab: A light switch is not a strategy.

sources.title: The International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA)

rebuttals.theySay: A fraud task force will finally stop waste!
rebuttals.youSay: GAO already tracks improper payments. A branded task force can help or become selective political enforcement, especially while Schedule Policy/Career remakes the career staff who catch waste. Process and independence matter more than press releases.
rebuttals.stab: Firing the referees and then announcing a fraud squad is not oversight.

sources.title: Establishing the Task Force To Eliminate Fraud
Implementing Schedule Policy/Career in the Excepted Service

rebuttals.theySay: Deregulating homebuilding will make housing cheap!
rebuttals.youSay: Zoning and permitting reform can help supply. Executive checklists that ignore local land-use, interest rates, and investor demand oversell a silver bullet. Affordability needs construction and demand-side honesty.
rebuttals.stab: A memo cannot print vacant apartments.

sources.title: Removing Regulatory Barriers to Affordable Home Construction
Stopping Wall Street From Competing With Main Street Homebuyers

rebuttals.theySay: Banning Wall Street from buying houses fixes the market!
rebuttals.youSay: Institutional single-family purchases matter in some metros. Nationwide affordability also tracks underbuilding, rates, wages, and zoning. One order against investors is not a housing plan.
rebuttals.stab: Scapegoats are cheaper than permits.

sources.title: Stopping Wall Street From Competing With Main Street Homebuyers

rebuttals.theySay: Extending the FEMA review council is just good management!
rebuttals.youSay: Disaster response capacity is life-or-death infrastructure. Keeping FEMA under open-ended political review creates uncertainty for states and survivors. Reform through statute and professional standards beats perpetual council theater.
rebuttals.stab: Hurricanes do not wait for another continuance notice.

sources.title: Further Continuance of the Federal Emergency Management Agency Review Council

rebuttals.theySay: Sharing felony records with DHS is basic safety!
rebuttals.youSay: Lawful information sharing for serious crimes is ordinary. An order expanding DHS criminal-history access for immigration dragnets can also sweep people with old, minor, or contested records into deportation pipelines. Scope and due process decide whether it is safety or sweep.
rebuttals.stab: Safety is precise. Dragnets are not.

sources.title: Protecting the National Security and Welfare of the United States and Its Citizens From Criminal Actors

rebuttals.theySay: An America First arms strategy keeps us safe!
rebuttals.youSay: Weapons-export strategy shapes wars others fight and blowback Americans inherit. America First branding does not answer end-use monitoring, human-rights conditions, or whether more transfers reduce or multiply conflict risk.
rebuttals.stab: Selling more weapons is not the same as having a strategy.

sources.title: Establishing an America First Arms Transfer Strategy

rebuttals.theySay: The Great American Recovery Initiative will end addiction!
rebuttals.youSay: Addiction policy needs treatment capacity, naloxone, housing, and evidence-based care. A branded initiative can fund those things or substitute slogans for beds. Outcomes beat naming ceremonies.
rebuttals.stab: Epidemics do not end at a logo unveil.

sources.title: Addressing Addiction Through the Great American Recovery Initiative

rebuttals.theySay: Truthful Made in America ads fix the trade deficit!
rebuttals.youSay: Truth-in-labeling helps consumers. It does not rebuild industrial capacity, rewrite tax incentives, or replace trade and labor policy. Do not confuse advertising enforcement with an industrial strategy.
rebuttals.stab: A honest label on an imported part is still an imported part.

sources.title: Ensuring Truthful Advertising of Products Claiming To Be Made in America

rebuttals.theySay: The 250th anniversary proves the founding was perfect!
rebuttals.youSay: Anniversaries are civic rituals. The Declaration's equality language coexisted with slavery and Indigenous dispossession. Celebrating ideals while refusing the contradictions is pageantry, not history.
rebuttals.stab: If the founding were finished, we would not need amendments.

sources.title: 250th Anniversary of the Adoption of the Declaration of Independence

rebuttals.theySay: Schedule Policy/Career is totally different from Schedule F!
rebuttals.youSay: Renaming an excepted-service conversion does not erase the core move: expanding at-will political removal risk for policy-influencing career roles. Read the Federal Register text, not the rebrand.
rebuttals.stab: If it walks like Schedule F and fires like Schedule F, the logo is not a shield.

sources.title: Implementing Schedule Policy/Career in the Excepted Service

rebuttals.theySay: NSPMs are normal national-security paperwork!
rebuttals.youSay: Presidents issue national-security memoranda. A cascade of NSPMs that remake policy outside notice-and-comment still concentrates power and reduces public visibility. Normal paperwork can still be abnormal governance.
rebuttals.stab: Classified memo velocity is not democratic consent.

rebuttals.theySay: Freedom to Fix is just right-to-repair for cars!
rebuttals.youSay: Right-to-repair can help consumers. An EPA-directed memo that deprioritizes emissions-tampering enforcement is also an air-quality and climate choice. Read the enforcement priorities, not only the slogan.
rebuttals.stab: If the win requires dirtier exhaust, say that part out loud.

sources.title: Lowering the Cost of Living by Promoting the Freedom to Fix

rebuttals.theySay: Duty-free Moroccan phosphate is a smart emergency!
rebuttals.youSay: Fertilizer costs matter to farms. Declaring emergencies to rewrite tariff treatment for a favored supply line is still emergency-powered trade policy. Ask who benefits, for how long, and what precedent it sets.
rebuttals.stab: If everything is an emergency, tariffs are a royal privilege.

sources.title: Declaration of Emergency and Authorization for Temporary Duty Free Importation of Phosphate Fertilizer Morocco

rebuttals.theySay: Adjusting aircraft imports is national security!
rebuttals.youSay: Aerospace supply chains are strategic. Section 232-style and proclamation trade tools still let presidents pick winners with thin public process. Security labeling does not make every tariff a treaty.
rebuttals.stab: National security is not a free pass to skip Congress forever.

sources.title: Adjusting Imports of Commercial Aircraft, Jet Engines, and Aircraft and Engine Parts into the United States

rebuttals.theySay: Restoring Pacific fishing just helps fishermen!
rebuttals.youSay: Fishing communities need sustainable access. A proclamation that reframes conservation constraints can also reopen ecological risk. Fisheries science, not nostalgia, decides whether stocks survive.
rebuttals.stab: Empty oceans are not a jobs program.

sources.title: Restoring American Commercial Fishing in the Pacific

rebuttals.theySay: Pardons are absolute so stop complaining!
rebuttals.youSay: The pardon power is broad. Using it for political allies still teaches that accountability is optional for the connected. Constitutional does not mean healthy for the rule of law.
rebuttals.stab: A legal pardon can still be a corrupt signal.

sources.title: Granting Pardon to Stephen E. Buyer

rebuttals.theySay: A regenerative agriculture order means climate policy is done!
rebuttals.youSay: Soil health practices can help. One agriculture order does not replace emissions rules, power-sector policy, or methane standards. Do not let a farm-branded EO retire the rest of the climate file.
rebuttals.stab: Cover crops are good. Cover stories are not climate policy.

sources.title: Advancing Regenerative Agriculture and Strengthening American Farm Resilience

rebuttals.theySay: Quantum and crypto orders are just tech competitiveness!
rebuttals.youSay: Advanced-tech industrial policy can be legitimate. Stacking cryptographic and quantum directives by executive order still concentrates dual-use tech governance without ordinary legislative bargaining. Competitiveness and oversight are not enemies.
rebuttals.stab: Moonshot memos still need sunlight.

sources.title: Ushering in the Next Frontier of Quantum Innovation
Securing the Nation Against Advanced Cryptographic Attacks
National Institute of Standards and Technology

rebuttals.theySay: Fintech innovation plus financial integrity orders protect consumers!
rebuttals.youSay: Innovation language paired with integrity branding can still mean lighter supervision. Watch CFPB and FTC enforcement capacity, not dual press releases. Consumer protection is staffing and cases, not adjectives.
rebuttals.stab: Integrity without enforcers is a brochure.

sources.title: Integrating Financial Technology Innovation into Regulatory Frameworks
Restoring Integrity to America's Financial System

rebuttals.theySay: AI innovation orders mean we must scrap safety rules!
rebuttals.youSay: Competitiveness and safety are not a binary. An AI innovation-and-security order can fund research and still leave accountability, labor, and civil-rights risks unaddressed. Deregulation is a choice, not a physics law.
rebuttals.stab: Move fast and break democracy is not a growth strategy.

sources.title: Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security
Office of Science and Technology Policy

rebuttals.theySay: Removing federal lands restrictions frees Americans!
rebuttals.youSay: Public lands are a shared inheritance. Removing access restrictions can mean more drilling, mining, and habitat loss under a freedom brand. Freedom for extractors is not automatically freedom for hikers, tribes, or future taxpayers.
rebuttals.stab: Selling the commons is not liberation.

sources.title: Removing Unnecessary and Counterproductive Restrictions on Access to Federal Lands

rebuttals.theySay: Realigning vaccines with peer countries is just science!
rebuttals.youSay: Schedule changes should follow transparent evidence review by independent medical bodies. An executive realignment order risks politicizing childhood immunization recommendations. Peer-country shopping is not a substitute for ACIP-style process.
rebuttals.stab: Science is a method, not a press release about Europe.

sources.title: Realigning United States Core Childhood Vaccine Recommendations With Best Practices From Peer, Developed Countries

rebuttals.theySay: Strengthening customs enforcement is only about trade!
rebuttals.youSay: Customs sits at the intersection of tariffs, drugs, and migration. An enforcement surge can be trade policy and interior hard-power at once. Read the operational directives, not the narrowest possible gloss.
rebuttals.stab: Ports of entry enforce more than invoices.

rebuttals.theySay: Federal contracting efficiency orders save taxpayer money!
rebuttals.youSay: Procurement reform can cut waste. Efficiency orders can also preference political vendors, weaken labor and equity clauses, and reduce oversight capacity. Savings claims need IG audits, not vibes.
rebuttals.stab: Efficiency is a spreadsheet word until the inspector general signs.

sources.title: Promoting Efficiency, Accountability, and Performance in Federal Contracting

rebuttals.theySay: Every president signs lots of executive orders so stop counting!
rebuttals.youSay: Volume is not content. Rescinding climate rules, converting civil servants, and rewriting election administration are not equivalent to commemorative proclamations. Compare substance, severity, and durability.
rebuttals.stab: A holiday proclamation is not Schedule Policy/Career.

rebuttals.theySay: FOIA backlogs are boring bureaucracy, not a scandal!
rebuttals.youSay: Slow FOIA is how governments hide in plain sight. When personnel purges and records-pressure rise together, delayed disclosure is a democracy cost, not a paperwork shrug.
rebuttals.stab: Sunshine delayed is sunlight denied.

rebuttals.theySay: Good jobs numbers prove every deregulatory move was right!
rebuttals.youSay: Monthly employment prints are one indicator. They do not validate Schedule Policy/Career, tariff volatility, or enforcement pauses. Correlation with a press cycle is not causation for every EO.
rebuttals.stab: A jobs print is not a moral blank check.

rebuttals.theySay: Project 2025 is a conspiracy theory!
rebuttals.youSay: Heritage published a public mandate. Personnel and policy overlaps are documentable in the Federal Register and White House actions. Denial after implementation is branding, not evidence.
rebuttals.stab: You can dislike the tracker. You cannot unread the PDFs.

rebuttals.theySay: Your severity scores are just liberal feelings!
rebuttals.youSay: Scores summarize impact scope using sourced actions, not vibes alone. You can dispute a number; you still have to answer the underlying Federal Register text and affected sectors.
rebuttals.stab: Arguing with the thermometer does not cool the room.

rebuttals.theySay: Teaching hard history is classroom indoctrination!
rebuttals.youSay: Primary sources from the National Archives, NPS, and Congress are not a party platform. Hiding massacres and labor wars is the indoctrination. Students can handle footnotes.
rebuttals.stab: If the document scares you, the problem is not the teenager.

rebuttals.theySay: Career officials are the deep state!
rebuttals.youSay: Career civil servants are the continuity layer that keeps Social Security checks, food inspections, and aviation safe across elections. Politicizing them raises corruption and competence risk. Disagreement is not a conspiracy.
rebuttals.stab: The deep state is often just someone who still has the manual.

rebuttals.theySay: Emergency powers are how strong leaders govern!
rebuttals.youSay: Time-limited emergencies can be necessary. Habitual emergency trade, immigration, and agency rewrites train the public to accept rule by proclamation. Strength without sunsets is just monarchy with better stationery.
rebuttals.stab: If the emergency never ends, the republic is the temporary measure.

rebuttals.theySay: More aluminum and steel tariffs mean more American factory jobs!
rebuttals.youSay: Metals proclamations can raise input costs for builders, automakers, and manufacturers while any hiring gains concentrate in protected niches. CRS and BLS treat tariff incidence as distributional, not a nationwide hiring miracle.
