rebuttals.stab: A tariff is a tax with a patriotism filter. Follow prices, not slogans.

rebuttals.theySay: Defense Production Act paperwork is too boring to care about!
rebuttals.youSay: DPA delegations decide which agencies can compel production, prioritize contracts, and waive requirements. Boring authorities are how industrial policy happens without a floor fight.
rebuttals.stab: If it can commandeer supply chains, it is not trivia.

rebuttals.theySay: Leaving international organizations just restores sovereignty!
rebuttals.youSay: Exit memos can abandon cooperation on health, labor, and security without Congress rewriting the underlying commitments in public. Sovereignty theater that blinds the government is not strength.
rebuttals.stab: You can leave a room and still need the fire code.

rebuttals.theySay: Denying one tribal permit is a local zoning spat!
rebuttals.youSay: Presidential permit denials for tribal projects sit at the intersection of Indigenous sovereignty, borders, and infrastructure. Calling it zoning hides the federal power move.
rebuttals.stab: If the White House stamps denied, it is not a HOA meeting.

rebuttals.theySay: A football executive order is harmless Americana!
rebuttals.youSay: Preserving America's Game plus the college-sports emergency order shows culture policy federalized into athletics governance. Harmless branding is still agenda-setting with a whistle.
rebuttals.stab: When the presidency coaches football, look for the civil-rights tradeoffs.

rebuttals.theySay: DPA waivers cut red tape so America can build!
rebuttals.youSay: Section 303 waivers suspend statutory requirements for selected priorities. Speed without disclosure is how industrial favors hide inside emergency grammar.
rebuttals.stab: Red tape is sometimes the part where the public gets a look.

rebuttals.theySay: Implementing appropriations by proclamation is just administration!
rebuttals.youSay: Spending bills are Congress's power of the purse. Selective presidential implementation can privilege favored readings while the receipt trail thins.
rebuttals.stab: If Congress writes the check, explain every endorsement stamp.

rebuttals.theySay: Critical pay authority just helps hire talent!
rebuttals.youSay: Pay flexibilities can be legitimate. Paired with Schedule Policy/Career removals, they also create a two-track workforce: prized hires up, career watchdogs out.
rebuttals.stab: Raise pay for experts. Do not confuse that with purging the referees.

rebuttals.theySay: Homeownership Month proves they care about housing!
rebuttals.youSay: Proclamations are calendar art. Housing outcomes track mortgage-credit orders, construction deregulation, and institutional-investor rules, not ribbon-cutting months.
rebuttals.stab: A hashtag month is not a rent payment.

sources.title: HUD
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

rebuttals.theySay: 240 events and 550 rebuttals are just vanity metrics!
rebuttals.youSay: Counts are mile markers for a receipt stack you can audit against Federal Register and White House links. Vanity is refusing to read event #240 because the number offends you.
rebuttals.stab: If documentation is vanity, secrecy must be virtue. Cute theory.

rebuttals.theySay: Withdrawal memos are not treaties so chill out!
rebuttals.youSay: Memoranda can still trigger exits, defunding, and nonparticipation that change facts on the ground. Formality is not the same as consequence.
rebuttals.stab: A memo that strands diplomacy is policy wearing slippers.

rebuttals.theySay: Every metals tariff is national security. End of story!
rebuttals.youSay: National-security justifications can be real or elastic. Congress and CRS still get to ask which blast furnaces and which threats match which duty rates.
rebuttals.stab: If everything is security, oversight is the enemy. That is the tell.

rebuttals.theySay: States should control tribal permits, not activists!
rebuttals.youSay: Federally recognized tribes have a government-to-government relationship with the United States. Presidential permit fights are federal Indian law, not a states-rights cosplay.
rebuttals.stab: Sovereignty is not a zoning variance you revoke for vibes.

rebuttals.theySay: Sports executive orders protect parents' rights!
rebuttals.youSay: Federal sports orders can override local athletic governance while Title IX and education civil-rights capacity shrink. Parents' rights branding does not audit the statute stack.
rebuttals.stab: If parents needed an EO to yell at a referee, the problem is elsewhere.

rebuttals.theySay: Industrial policy is socialism unless our guy does it!
rebuttals.youSay: DPA tools, tariffs, and contracting directives are industrial policy regardless of the scarf color. Debate the targets and transparency, not the existence of the toolbox.
rebuttals.stab: Socialism is when the out-group uses the same authorities.

rebuttals.theySay: FOIA still works, stop whining about backlogs!
rebuttals.youSay: Statutory rights without timely production are parchment promises. DOJ FOIA dashboards and GAO audits exist because delay is a known failure mode.
rebuttals.stab: A right you wait five years to use is a suggestion.

rebuttals.theySay: Schedule Policy/Career is just HR modernization!
rebuttals.youSay: Excepted-service conversions change removal protections. MSPB and OPM materials still describe merit-system principles that politicized firings threaten.
rebuttals.stab: HR modernization that makes loyalty the KPI is a purge with benefits paperwork.

rebuttals.theySay: Tariffs will crush inflation for families!
rebuttals.youSay: Import taxes can raise prices of goods and inputs. BLS CPI does not care about campaign poetry when washing machines and lumber cost more.
rebuttals.stab: Taxing imports is not a coupon for groceries.

rebuttals.theySay: Leaving organizations saves taxpayer money!
rebuttals.youSay: Dues are visible; lost cooperation on pandemics, standards, and security is not a line item until the crisis invoice arrives. CBO-style budgeting still asks about net risk.
rebuttals.stab: The cheapest membership fee is the one you pay before the disaster.

rebuttals.theySay: Federal Register links are partisan gotchas!
rebuttals.youSay: The Federal Register is the government's own journal. Quoting it is not spin; refusing to read it is.
rebuttals.stab: If primary sources feel partisan, check which team fears sunlight.

rebuttals.theySay: Denying the Kickapoo permit protects the border!
rebuttals.youSay: Border security and tribal self-determination are not automatic opposites. A permit denial still requires a public rationale beyond vibes.
rebuttals.stab: Security without consultation is just domination with better lighting.

rebuttals.theySay: Critics only hate the football order because they hate America!
rebuttals.youSay: Critics track presidential bandwidth. Sports EOs arriving beside Schedule Policy/Career and tariff stacks show agenda priorities, not team jerseys.
rebuttals.stab: America is not a halftime show for deregulation.

rebuttals.theySay: Presidents waive statutes all the time!
rebuttals.youSay: Some waiver clauses exist by design. Habitually leaning on them for industrial favorites still deserves GAO and congressional scrutiny.
rebuttals.stab: Common abuse is still abuse.

rebuttals.theySay: Appropriations text is more like guidelines!
rebuttals.youSay: The Constitution puts the purse in Congress. Implementation proclamations that cherry-pick invite impoundment-by-another-name fights.
rebuttals.stab: Guidelines is what kings call statutes.

rebuttals.theySay: Critical pay for national-security talent is nonnegotiable!
rebuttals.youSay: Competing for talent matters. So does keeping career analysts who can say no. Pay up without converting the civil service into a patronage ladder.
rebuttals.stab: Brain gain plus loyalty tests is how you lose the brains that dissent.

rebuttals.theySay: Only supply matters - deregulate everything!
rebuttals.youSay: Supply constraints are real. So are credit rules, investor purchases, zoning politics, and wage stagnation. HUD and Census data support a multi-cause diagnosis.
rebuttals.stab: One-knob economics breaks when the machine has twelve knobs.

sources.title: HUD
sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

rebuttals.theySay: Lexington and Concord were only about taxes!
rebuttals.youSay: NPS histories cover arms seizures, imperial authority, and colonial resistance. Reducing 1775 to a modern tax meme flattens the record.
rebuttals.stab: If your founding story fits on a bumper sticker, it is incomplete.

rebuttals.theySay: Custer was a hero ambushed by savages!
rebuttals.youSay: NPS interpretation of Little Bighorn treats it as a battle in a larger dispossession campaign. Hero mythology erased Indigenous strategy for generations.
rebuttals.stab: Losing a battle does not mint a saint.

rebuttals.theySay: Sacco and Vanzetti were guilty, case closed!
rebuttals.youSay: Historians still debate evidence while agreeing nativism and Red Scare politics tainted the atmosphere. Case closed is how miscarriages market themselves.
rebuttals.stab: If the trial needs a century of defense briefs, it was not tidy.

rebuttals.theySay: Hiroshima debate is unpatriotic!
rebuttals.youSay: National Archives documentation of the bombings exists so citizens can weigh military claims against civilian cost. Patriotism that bans archives is cosplay.
rebuttals.stab: If the decision was airtight, primary sources would not scare you.

rebuttals.theySay: Social Security was perfectly color-blind from day one!
rebuttals.youSay: SSA history notes occupational exclusions that left many Black, domestic, and agricultural workers outside early coverage. Design details matter.
rebuttals.stab: Color-blind is easy to claim when the exclusions are occupational.

rebuttals.theySay: Shelby County just updated an outdated coverage map!
rebuttals.youSay: Striking preclearance without a replacement formula predicted and produced new voting barriers. Brennan Center and election litigators tracked the wave in real time.
rebuttals.stab: Updating a map by deleting the referee is not modernization.

rebuttals.theySay: Citizens United is just free speech!
rebuttals.youSay: Unlimited independent spending amplifies treasury-sized speakers. FEC disclosures and OpenSecrets charts show the flood after 2010.
rebuttals.stab: A megaphone the size of a bank balance is not a town hall.

rebuttals.theySay: Guantanamo is still needed for the worst of the worst!
rebuttals.youSay: Indefinite detention without normal trials created a due-process crisis the U.S. still pays for in courts and credibility. Worst-of-the-worst rhetoric is not a substitute for charges.
rebuttals.stab: If the evidence is solid, use a courtroom. If it is not, that is the scandal.

rebuttals.theySay: Nobody could have helped Puerto Rico after Maria!
rebuttals.youSay: Storms are natural; response capacity, colonial status, and aid logistics are policy. Death-toll disputes were about counting, not magic.
rebuttals.stab: Paper towels are not a logistics plan.

rebuttals.theySay: Bush v. Gore was pure law, not politics!
rebuttals.youSay: A 5-4 halt to recounts decided the presidency. Equal-protection reasoning limited to that case is why critics call it politics in robes.
rebuttals.stab: When the remedy is stop counting, bring receipts, not incense.

rebuttals.theySay: Korea was a minor police action!
rebuttals.youSay: National Archives military records document a devastating war without a peace treaty. Footnotes do not kill millions.
rebuttals.stab: If it still needs troops on a parallel today, it was not minor.

rebuttals.theySay: The Marshall Plan was pure charity!
rebuttals.youSay: State Department histories describe strategic containment and market rebuilding alongside humanitarian aims. Strategy and solidarity can coexist.
rebuttals.stab: Charity that rebuilds allies is still strategy. Own it.

rebuttals.theySay: Chicago 1968 was hippies attacking police!
rebuttals.youSay: Televised police violence against demonstrators is documented across news archives and later commissions. Clubbing protesters is not a debate tournament.
rebuttals.stab: If your cut of the tape starts after the baton, you are editing.

rebuttals.theySay: 1973 was just Arab greed!
rebuttals.youSay: Federal Reserve history essays cover embargo politics, price shocks, and U.S. energy dependence. Monocausal greed stories skip policy choices.
rebuttals.stab: Scapegoats are cheaper than energy policy.

rebuttals.theySay: Iran took hostages because they hate freedom!
rebuttals.youSay: The 1953 coup and the Shah's U.S. medical haven are part of the documentary record. Hatred slogans erase causation you can read in State Department histories.
rebuttals.stab: Amnesia is not a foreign policy.

rebuttals.theySay: The S&L crisis was only a few bad apples!
rebuttals.youSay: Federal Reserve history ties thrift deregulation and supervision failures to a taxpayer cleanup. Apples rot faster when you remove the fridge.
rebuttals.stab: Bailouts are how you meet the orchard.

rebuttals.theySay: The NAACP was outside agitators stirring trouble!
rebuttals.youSay: Founded after racial terror organizing, the NAACP used litigation and advocacy against Jim Crow for decades. Agitator is what segregationists called plaintiffs.
rebuttals.stab: If equality needs a lawyer, the calm people were the problem.

rebuttals.theySay: The Federal Reserve is an illegal deep-state bank!
rebuttals.youSay: Congress wrote the Federal Reserve Act in 1913 after panics. You can demand more accountability without pretending it spawned from a volcano.
rebuttals.stab: Statutory agencies are not lizard people. Read the act.

rebuttals.theySay: Gettysburg and the Address healed the nation!
rebuttals.youSay: NPS sites teach the battle and the speech as unfinished work toward equality. Reconciliation myths that skip Black freedom fights are fan service.
rebuttals.stab: Two minutes of poetry did not finish Reconstruction.

rebuttals.theySay: Appomattox ended the race issue!
rebuttals.youSay: Surrender ended a field army. Black Codes, terror, and the Compromise of 1877 show the citizenship fight continued.
rebuttals.stab: A ceasefire is not a civil rights act.

rebuttals.theySay: The caning of Sumner was both sides being violent!
rebuttals.youSay: A House member beat an antislavery senator on the Senate floor. That is not a debate club tie.
rebuttals.stab: If your symmetry needs a cane, it is not symmetry.

rebuttals.theySay: America already had democracy by the 1840s!
rebuttals.youSay: The Dorr Rebellion erupted because Rhode Island's charter still restricted the vote. Even white working men had to fight for ballots.
rebuttals.stab: Finished democracies do not need rebellions over who counts.

rebuttals.theySay: America defeated Britain alone at Yorktown!
rebuttals.youSay: NPS Yorktown interpretation centers the Franco-American alliance. Solo-hero myths erase French ships and troops.
rebuttals.stab: Allies are not a plot against exceptionalism.

rebuttals.theySay: 1800 proves transfers of power are always smooth!
rebuttals.youSay: House deadlock nearly broke the system. Peaceful transfer was improvisation under pressure, not destiny.
rebuttals.stab: A near-miss is not a warranty.

rebuttals.theySay: The 1964 Civil Rights Act ended racism!
rebuttals.youSay: The Act banned legal Jim Crow in public accommodations and employment. Enforcement fights and backlash never vanished from court dockets.
rebuttals.stab: A statute can ban segregation and still leave discrimination to kill.

rebuttals.theySay: There cannot possibly be more verifiable actions to log!
rebuttals.youSay: Federal Register and White House feeds keep publishing. Pass 30 added DPA waivers, metals tariffs, treaty-exit memos, and tribal permit fights because the receipt printer did not jam.
rebuttals.stab: Infinite room is just another way to say the record is still live.

rebuttals.theySay: Demanding primary sources is elitist!
rebuttals.youSay: Archives, CRS, GAO, and agency dashboards are public. Elitism is telling voters to trust vibes while the PDFs sit online.
rebuttals.stab: Literacy is not a coastal plot.

rebuttals.theySay: Severity scores are made-up activism!
rebuttals.youSay: Scores summarize documented impact across sectors with linked sources. Argue the rubric; do not pretend the underlying EO vanished.
rebuttals.stab: If scoring offends you, try refuting the citation instead.

rebuttals.theySay: Both sides issue the same volume of executive actions!
rebuttals.youSay: Volume can be compared. Content still differs: Schedule Policy/Career, citizenship-verification elections orders, and treaty-exit memos are not generic stationery.
rebuttals.stab: Counting pages without reading them is how both-sides cosplay works.

rebuttals.theySay: America was founded as a Christian nation!
rebuttals.youSay: The Senate-ratified Treaty of Tripoli said the opposite in 1797. Founding law is not a church pamphlet.
rebuttals.stab: If your theology needs you to ignore a ratified treaty, it is not history.

rebuttals.theySay: The Indian Citizenship Act finished Native equality!
rebuttals.youSay: Citizenship in 1924 did not restore land or guarantee ballots. States kept blocking Native voters for decades.
rebuttals.stab: A status stamp is not sovereignty.

rebuttals.theySay: The Hollywood blacklist was just studio taste!
rebuttals.youSay: HUAC hearings and secret employment bans destroyed careers over politics without convictions.
rebuttals.stab: Taste that ends your paycheck is a blacklist.

rebuttals.theySay: Interracial marriage was always obviously legal!
rebuttals.youSay: States criminalized it until Loving v. Virginia in 1967. Courts had to kill the bans.
rebuttals.stab: Obvious now was illegal then.

rebuttals.theySay: The Church Committee was just a smear job!
rebuttals.youSay: Senate investigators documented COINTELPRO, assassination plots, and illegal surveillance with primary records.
rebuttals.stab: Calling oversight a smear is how covert programs survive.

rebuttals.theySay: FOIA is optional political theater!
rebuttals.youSay: FOIA is statute. Agencies stall because disclosure is mandatory, not because vibes demand secrecy.
rebuttals.stab: If it were optional, backlogs would not need excuses.

rebuttals.theySay: War Powers is irrelevant theater!
rebuttals.youSay: Congress wrote it after Vietnam because unilateral force kept expanding. Compliance fights prove it still matters.
rebuttals.stab: Theater does not get joint resolutions.

rebuttals.theySay: Three Mile Island was a nothingburger!
rebuttals.youSay: A partial meltdown froze U.S. reactor construction and exposed emergency-communication failure. NRC still publishes the case.
rebuttals.stab: Nothingburgers do not rewrite an industry.

rebuttals.theySay: Exxon Valdez was one unlucky captain!
rebuttals.youSay: Weak tanker oversight and corporate cost-cutting produced an ecological catastrophe. Double-hull reforms came after.
rebuttals.stab: Luck is not a spill-prevention plan.

rebuttals.theySay: The ADA is special rights!
rebuttals.youSay: Access ramps and job accommodations are equal citizenship, not VIP passes. Congress passed a civil-rights statute.
rebuttals.stab: Calling a ramp special is the tell.

rebuttals.theySay: 1996 welfare reform ended poverty!
rebuttals.youSay: Caseloads fell; deep poverty and hardship metrics did not magically vanish. Time limits are not a jobs program.
rebuttals.stab: A closed casefile is not a full fridge.

rebuttals.theySay: Gramm-Leach-Bliley had nothing to do with 2008!
rebuttals.youSay: Repealing Glass-Steagall barriers enabled megabank consolidation that amplified systemic risk before the crash.
rebuttals.stab: If firewalls never mattered, why rebuild pieces afterward?

rebuttals.theySay: Enron was a few bad apples!
rebuttals.youSay: Fraud plus deregulated energy markets wiped pensions and forced Sarbanes-Oxley. Systems failed, not just morals.
rebuttals.stab: Bad-apple talk ends where retirees go broke.

rebuttals.theySay: Deepwater Horizon was a freak accident!
rebuttals.youSay: Eleven dead workers and millions of barrels followed safety failures and weak oversight, not a lightning strike.
rebuttals.stab: Freak accidents do not need blowout-preventer hearings.

rebuttals.theySay: Snowden was only a traitor, full stop!
rebuttals.youSay: Whatever you think of his flight, the documents showed bulk collection on Americans. The Fourth Amendment debate is the point.
rebuttals.stab: Calling the messenger a traitor does not delete the slides.

rebuttals.theySay: Ferguson was only outside agitators!
rebuttals.youSay: DOJ documented unconstitutional policing and court revenue schemes in Ferguson. Federal findings are not a Twitter mob.
rebuttals.stab: Agitators do not write DOJ pattern-or-practice reports.

rebuttals.theySay: Fox was just asking questions about Dominion!
rebuttals.youSay: Fox paid $787.5 million after airing claims it knew were false. Settlements that size are not curiosity.
rebuttals.stab: Questions that cost nearly a billion are libel with a receipt.

rebuttals.theySay: Presidents always had total criminal immunity!
rebuttals.youSay: Trump v. United States (2024) newly broadened official-act immunity. Nixon's world was not this holding.
rebuttals.stab: If it always existed, the Court would not have needed a landmark rewrite.

rebuttals.theySay: America stayed isolationist after WWII!
rebuttals.youSay: NATO in 1949 created permanent peacetime commitments. Isolation ended on paper with Article 5.
rebuttals.stab: Alliances are not isolation with better branding.

rebuttals.theySay: Teapot Dome proves both sides are always identical!
rebuttals.youSay: A specific bribery scandal does not make every ethics fight cosplay. Name the leases and the money.
rebuttals.stab: Both-sides is not a documentary.

rebuttals.theySay: The Wagner Act made unions un-American!
rebuttals.youSay: Congress protected organizing after private armies killed strikers. Collective bargaining is statute, not sedition.
rebuttals.stab: Calling the NLRA un-American is lobbying, not patriotism.

rebuttals.theySay: Camps like Manzanar protected Japanese Americans!
rebuttals.youSay: Armed confinement without charges stole homes and businesses. Protection does not post sentries at the gate.
rebuttals.stab: If it were protection, people could leave.

rebuttals.theySay: Alcatraz was just publicity!
rebuttals.youSay: Nineteen months of occupation demanded treaty enforcement. NPS still interprets it as a sovereignty protest.
rebuttals.stab: Publicity stunts do not outlast winter on a rock.

rebuttals.theySay: Ruby Ridge was fully justified!
rebuttals.youSay: Rules-of-engagement failures killed civilians and fueled militia radicalization. Tactics matter when the state holds the rifles.
rebuttals.stab: Justified operations do not recruit the next bombing.

rebuttals.theySay: Serious terror is always foreign!
rebuttals.youSay: Oklahoma City killed 168 people. Domestic extremism is not a myth you can deport.
rebuttals.stab: Body counts do not check passports first.

rebuttals.theySay: Crack and powder disparities were fair!
rebuttals.youSay: A 100:1 ratio produced racial sentencing gaps Congress later partially repaired. Fair was the branding, not the math.
rebuttals.stab: If it were fair, reform would not have been needed.

rebuttals.theySay: Henrietta Lacks is a non-issue because rules differed!
rebuttals.youSay: HeLa built modern medicine without consent. NIH later negotiated privacy because the injustice was real.
rebuttals.stab: Different era is not a moral eraser.

sources.title: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

rebuttals.theySay: The Great Migration was just free-market choice!
rebuttals.youSay: People fled lynching and debt peonage. Northern redlining proved arrival was not equal welcome.
rebuttals.stab: Choice under terror is not a labor-market TED talk.

rebuttals.theySay: Debs deserved prison for hurting the war!
rebuttals.youSay: Antiwar speech is not espionage. The Espionage Act was used to cage dissent, and he ran for president from prison.
rebuttals.stab: If speech is sabotage, the First Amendment is a joke.

rebuttals.theySay: There cannot be more verifiable room after Pass 30!

rebuttals.youSay: Converting career civil servants into at-will political staff guts expertise and invites corruption. GAO flagged the risks.
rebuttals.stab: Loyalty oaths are not merit systems.

rebuttals.stab: Always is not a trade model.

rebuttals.stab: Normal is what Congress writes. Waivers are the exception lane.

rebuttals.youSay: Blanket withdrawal memos can dump treaties and forums without replacing the cooperation they provided.
rebuttals.stab: Sovereignty is not walking out of every room and calling it strategy.

rebuttals.stab: Local spats do not need White House letterhead.

rebuttals.youSay: Proclamations on Wall Street buyers and mortgage access do not build units. Supply, zoning, and subsidy still matter.

rebuttals.theySay: Citizenship verification for elections is common sense!
rebuttals.youSay: Poorly designed federal mandates risk blocking lawful voters and echoing the Shelby-era restriction wave.

rebuttals.theySay: Banning DEI for contractors is neutral!
rebuttals.youSay: Orders targeting equity programs chill lawful nondiscrimination work and invite politicized procurement.

rebuttals.theySay: Removing federal-lands restrictions is freedom!
rebuttals.youSay: Access rollbacks can open extraction and damage while branding deregulation as recreation.
rebuttals.stab: Freedom for bulldozers is not wilderness policy.

rebuttals.theySay: AI needs speed, not safety rules!
rebuttals.youSay: Innovation orders that skip enforceable baselines export risk to workers and the public.
rebuttals.stab: Speed without brakes is how you meet the wall first.

rebuttals.theySay: Washington should federalize college sports!
rebuttals.youSay: Preserving America's Game and related orders put culture politics in athletic governance.
rebuttals.stab: If football needs the Oval Office, the problem is not the playbook.

rebuttals.theySay: Endless FEMA review is responsible caution!
rebuttals.youSay: Indefinite review while climate losses rise can stall capacity under the banner of reform.

rebuttals.youSay: Concentrating fraud narrative power without strong IG independence risks selective enforcement.
rebuttals.stab: Trust requires inspectors general, not slogans.

rebuttals.stab: Morality that never updates is a press release.

rebuttals.youSay: Schools, contracts, and relief after slavery are governance, not waste. Starving the Bureau was the political project.

rebuttals.youSay: Poll taxes, literacy tests, and terror nullified paper rights for generations.

rebuttals.youSay: It shifted opinion; entry still took years, loans, and Zimmermann.
rebuttals.stab: One cause is lazy history.

rebuttals.theySay: The Zimmermann Telegram was fake news!
rebuttals.youSay: Archives hold the diplomatic text. Denial is not a primary source.
rebuttals.stab: Read the telegram.

rebuttals.theySay: Prohibition proves moral bans always work!
rebuttals.youSay: Black markets and repeal say enforcement without legitimacy fails.
rebuttals.stab: Speakeasies graded the law.

rebuttals.theySay: Rejecting Versailles kept America pure!
rebuttals.youSay: Isolation still left loans, empires, and a later world war.
rebuttals.stab: Purity is not a strategy.

rebuttals.theySay: Sit-down strikes were pure thuggery!
rebuttals.youSay: Occupying GM forced UAW recognition after starvation wages.
rebuttals.stab: Manners do not pay rent.

rebuttals.theySay: Four Freedoms were empty slogans!
rebuttals.youSay: They shaped Atlantic Charter language and postwar rights talk.
rebuttals.stab: Slogans steered treaties.

rebuttals.theySay: Bretton Woods was free markets alone!
rebuttals.youSay: Governments designed the IMF and World Bank. Architecture is statecraft.
rebuttals.stab: Markets did not convene themselves.

rebuttals.theySay: EO 9981 ended military racism overnight!
rebuttals.youSay: Orders start processes; unequal treatment persisted for years.
rebuttals.stab: Paper is not culture.

rebuttals.theySay: There was no Cold War militarization plan!
rebuttals.youSay: NSC-68 urged permanent buildup in writing.
rebuttals.stab: The memo exists.

rebuttals.theySay: Covert wars are cost-free!
rebuttals.youSay: Bay of Pigs failed on television. Blowback is a cost.
rebuttals.stab: Covert still burns.

rebuttals.theySay: Gulf of Tonkin was a narrow vote!
rebuttals.youSay: It became a blank check for Vietnam escalation.
rebuttals.stab: Narrow was the brochure.

rebuttals.theySay: Kerner ignored Black pathology!
rebuttals.youSay: The commission blamed white racism and segregation in plain English.
rebuttals.stab: Pathology was the dodge.

rebuttals.theySay: The ERA was unnecessary!
rebuttals.youSay: If equality was finished, ratification would have been easy.
rebuttals.stab: Opposition proved the gap.

rebuttals.theySay: Prop 13 cut taxes with no service tradeoff!
rebuttals.youSay: Caps without replacement revenue cut schools and local capacity.
rebuttals.stab: Arithmetic is undefeated.

rebuttals.theySay: PATCO was a one-off labor spat!
rebuttals.youSay: Mass firings signaled open season on public-sector unions.
rebuttals.stab: Employers heard the shot.

rebuttals.theySay: Contract with America was just process reform!
rebuttals.youSay: It pledged welfare cuts and deregulation. Process was branding.
rebuttals.stab: Read the promises.

rebuttals.theySay: 1996 welfare reform ended poverty!
rebuttals.youSay: It ended AFDC entitlement; poverty cycles and state gaps remained.
rebuttals.stab: Caseloads are not dignity.

rebuttals.theySay: The 2001 AUMF was temporary!
rebuttals.youSay: Administrations reused it for years of global operations.
rebuttals.stab: Temporary became doctrine.

rebuttals.theySay: The surge proved Iraq was won!
rebuttals.youSay: More troops is not a verdict; costs outlasted the slogan.
rebuttals.stab: Surge is not closure.

rebuttals.theySay: Bruen history settles every gun law!
rebuttals.youSay: Selective analogues become a judicial veto, not a time machine.
rebuttals.stab: History is sampled.

rebuttals.theySay: 360 history entries should be the ceiling!
rebuttals.youSay: Verifiable room is not a lid. Documents keep existing whether curricula catch up or not.
rebuttals.stab: Ceilings are political.

rebuttals.theySay: 320 tracker events are noise!
rebuttals.youSay: Pattern visibility requires continuity receipts. Noise is what power calls a stack.
rebuttals.stab: Volume can be signal.

rebuttals.theySay: 750 rebuttals are spam!
rebuttals.youSay: Talking points recycle; sourced counters scale with the recycle rate.
rebuttals.stab: Repetition is their strategy.

rebuttals.theySay: Pass 34 should stop expanding!
rebuttals.youSay: If claims remain verifiable, the desk keeps filing. Stop is a preference, not a standard.
rebuttals.stab: Infinite room, receipts required.

rebuttals.theySay: Both sides did Schedule F at this scale!
rebuttals.youSay: Show the comparable mass at-will conversion order. Slogans are not FOIA results.
rebuttals.stab: Both-sides needs PDFs.

rebuttals.theySay: Tariffs are a free lunch for workers!
rebuttals.youSay: Input costs rise for manufacturers and consumers. Lunch has a receipt.
rebuttals.stab: Prices are not slogans.

rebuttals.theySay: DPA waivers are just normal peacetime tools!
rebuttals.youSay: Industrial directing without transparency baselines is emergency politics as default.
rebuttals.stab: Normal needs sunlight.

rebuttals.theySay: Housing EOs alone fix shelter!
rebuttals.youSay: Stacked proclamations are not durable statute or funding.
rebuttals.stab: Orders are not roofs.

rebuttals.theySay: Citizenship verification mandates are harmless!
rebuttals.youSay: Compliance burdens and error risk fall on eligible voters and local clerks.
rebuttals.stab: Harmless is not a metric.

rebuttals.theySay: Indefinite FEMA review is fine!
rebuttals.youSay: Disaster governance under endless review is delay dressed as diligence.
rebuttals.stab: Review is not response.

rebuttals.theySay: FOIA backlogs are inevitable!
rebuttals.youSay: Backlogs are management choices about staffing and exemptions culture.
rebuttals.stab: Inevitable is a shrug.

rebuttals.theySay: Lands deregulation is just local control!
rebuttals.youSay: Federal defaults still steer extraction vs conservation at national scale.
rebuttals.stab: Local talk, federal levers.

rebuttals.theySay: Leaving international orgs is pure sovereignty!
rebuttals.youSay: Exit memos still need public accounting for alliances and obligations dumped.
rebuttals.stab: Sovereignty is not secrecy.

rebuttals.theySay: Kickapoo permitting is a one-off!
rebuttals.youSay: Indigenous projects hitting federal choke points is a recurring pattern, not a fluke.
rebuttals.stab: One-off needs evidence.

rebuttals.theySay: Federal sports branding is harmless!
rebuttals.youSay: It inserts national politics into athletics governance fights.
rebuttals.stab: Brand is not neutral.

rebuttals.theySay: Fraud task forces prove results by existing!
rebuttals.youSay: Without public metrics, branding outruns audit.
rebuttals.stab: Existence is not evidence.

rebuttals.theySay: Rebranding the IRA erases the statute!
rebuttals.youSay: Statutes remain until Congress repeals them. Branding is politics, not deletion.
rebuttals.stab: Dotgov is not repeal.

rebuttals.theySay: Customs toggles are stable policy!
rebuttals.youSay: Presidential de minimis flips are the opposite of stable statute.
rebuttals.stab: Toggle is the tell.

rebuttals.theySay: Appropriations-by-proclamation is fine!
rebuttals.youSay: Spending execution outside committee sunlight weakens legislative power of the purse.
rebuttals.stab: Purse needs daylight.

rebuttals.theySay: Critical-pay memos are harmless HR!
rebuttals.youSay: Standing exception tracks still deserve public criteria and oversight.
rebuttals.stab: HR can be power.
